The text is laid out over seven lines, somehwat unevenly spaced and sized, largest in lines 3-5, smallest in the final line, with some use of interpuncts.
Letters tending in some cases towards cursive (e.g. M), with I and T indistinguishable, E and F tall and narrow. Wedge shaped serifs at stroke ends.

Single line of letters below the moulding across the top of the face.
The final o is much smaller (55 mm), the letters have a relatively tall and narrow module.
Single surviving line of Latin letters, with vacat before.
The text is centered on the upper half of the face of the stone with a uniform distribution but lacks a careful layout (ordinatio).
Epitaph of Saturninus erected by Canace
Text over two lines across the face of the stone; the second line is somewhat irregular in spacing and height.
epitaph of saturninus erected by canace
Epitaph of Cassianus erected by his son Cassius
The text is laid out in a somewhat irregular form over four lines.
epitaph of cassianus erected by his son cassius
Two lines of deeply cut, somewhat irregular letters, on the upper part of the front face of the stele, with the second line somewhat compressed at the end.
Somewhat irregular plain letters, with the T of line 2 extended (120mm), the penultimate I in ligature, and the final S small to fit in.
In the cloister of the Basilica Concattedrale di San Bartolomeo, Lipari
Text is roughly centered on the upper half of the stele, with an area of erasure below. The lines are somewhat irregular in their layout.
Ferrua, 1941, Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana
Epitaph erected by Hilara for her daughter
Text over two lines, large somewhat irregular letters across the middle of the front face.
epitaph erected by hilara for her daughter
Epitaph of Aulus Hirtius Iunianus and Tucuria Alcimilla
The text is approximately centred over 5 lines, with an incised horizontal margin below the inscription.
Currently in the Norman cloister of the castle
epitaph of aulus hirtius iunianus and tucuria alcimilla
The text is centered on the stone, filling the available field, with large if slightly irregular letters and spacing.
Letters are tall, with straight horizontal serifs, and curved strokes to the X; I is extended and final O on line 1 is small.
Two lines of Latin letters across the middle of the front of the stone, somewhat irregularly laid out.
The P and R in line 2 are much larger than the other letters.
Epitaph of Caius Publicius Clarus Auctus
The text is deeply carved on the projecting lower part of the front face of the plaque, set out somewhat irregularly over two lines.
Deep, relatively plain and uneven letters of irregular size.
epitaph of caius publicius clarus auctus
